zotero.abstractNote: Deep neural networks are known to be overconfident on out-of-distribution data. While many heuristic methods have been proposed to detect such data, they often fail on adversarial examples. In this paper, we propose a method to train neural networks that are provably certain on a given set of training data and have a guaranteed uncertainty on the complement of the training data. We achieve this by training the network to be constant on the training data and to have a high uncertainty on the complement. We show that our method is effective in detecting out-of-distribution data and is robust to adversarial attacks.
